title: On keeping a slow archive.
summary: A short essay about arranging images, film, and writing into an editorial space instead of a feed.
deck: Why some things are better kept in a structure than in a stream.
body: Feeds are fast. Archives are slower.
This journal entry is a note on why personal work benefits from being arranged, revisited, and read in context, rather than simply posted and pushed away.

title: Notes from a rainy day.
summary: A short note about sound, streets, and windows on a day when the city felt slightly slower.
body: On some days, the city is defined by the sound of rain rather than traffic.
This note keeps a small record of that feeling.
